narrative_md: Each FHIR Location maps to one OMOP location row representing a physical address. Location.address fields map to address_1, address_2, city, state, zip, county, and country. Location.position maps to latitude/longitude. The location table is shared with Patient.address -- both populate the same table and rows should be deduplicated by address components to avoid duplicates.

fields.notes: Surrogate key. Each unique address should be assigned a unique key. fhir-to-omop-demo uses Location.id directly.

sources.comment: Uses Location.id verbatim as the location_id integer
Address-based lookup-or-create via AddressUtil.searchAndUpdate(); returns existing location_id if address already present

references.notes: AddressUtil.searchAndUpdate() on Organization.address — no standalone Location resource handling

sources.comment: Single dummy location (id=1) for all unresolved references — no real address mapping

references.notes: Creates single dummy location row id=1

fields.notes: Second line of street address. If more than 2 address lines, concatenate third+ into address_2 or drop them.
State. May need normalization (e.g., 'California' -> 'CA'). varchar(2) may force lossy mapping for international locations.
3-, 5-, or 9-digit US zip; international codes also allowed. Truncate if >9 chars.
FHIR district is the closest analog to OMOP county.
Verbatim source identifier or full address text (Location.address.text) for traceability.

sources.comment: Uses Location.id as the source value for traceability
Uses full address text (Location.address.text) as source value alternative

references.notes: AddressUtil handles source value from address components

fields.notes: Mapped to OMOP Geography domain concept. 0 if unmapped.

sources.comment: Maps country string to Geography domain concept_id; defaults to 0 when no mapping found

references.notes: Creates both a location row and a care_site row from Location resource. Location row: address fields + lat/lng.
AddressUtil.searchAndUpdate() resolves addresses from Organization.address into location rows. No standalone Location resource handling.
Creates single dummy location (id=1) for all unresolved references.

edge_cases.handling: OMOP location has only address_1 and address_2. Concatenate the third+ lines into address_2 or drop them.
Create a location row with only latitude/longitude populated; address fields null.
latitude and longitude null. OMOP allows nulls.
Normalize (e.g., 'California' -> 'CA'). The OMOP state field is varchar(2). For international locations, the 2-char limit may force lossy mapping.
Truncate to 9 characters or store in location_source_value.

edge_cases.case: Country present but no concept mapping

edge_cases.handling: Reject or null. OMOP DDL constraints in some dialects enforce this.

edge_cases.case: Same address appearing in both Patient.address and Location
edge_cases.handling: Deduplicate by (address_1, city, state, zip). Re-use existing location_id.

edge_cases.case: Multiple Locations with identical addresses but different Location.id
edge_cases.handling: Choose one canonical location row; map both Location.id values to it via the ID-mapping table.
Used to derive a care_site row in the dual-source pattern. See Location__care_site edge.
